summary:
Design a way to turn a binary tree into a string (serialize) and to rebuild the identical tree back from that string (deserialize).

You choose the encoding format yourself; the only requirement is that serializing a tree and then deserializing the result reproduces the original tree exactly, with the same structure and values. In this exercise your function takes a tree, serializes it, deserializes that output, and returns the reconstructed tree so the round trip can be checked.

examples.explanation: After serializing to a string and deserializing it back, the rebuilt tree matches the original structure and values.
An empty tree round-trips to an empty tree.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4].
Deserializing the serialized output must rebuild a tree identical to the input.
An empty tree must round-trip back to an empty tree.
